independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for scaffolding tasks)

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py` defines a module-level attribute `_stub_response_hook: Callable[[CommandPayload], StubResponse] | None = None`.
criteria_verification.evidence: src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py defines `_stub_response_hook: Callable[[CommandPayload], StubResponse] | None = None` at module scope (line ~92) with the documented default of None. CommandPayload is imported from nats_core.events; the StubResponse alias precedes the attribute. Three tests (TestAC001StubResponseHookAttribute) verify (a) the attribute exists and defaults to None, (b) the type-hint introspects to Optional[Callable[[CommandPayload], StubResponse]], and (c) it is writable at runtime so test fixtures can monkeypatch it.
criteria_verification.notes: src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py defines `_stub_response_hook: Callable[[CommandPayload], StubResponse] | None = None` at module scope (line ~92) with the documented default of None. CommandPayload is imported from nats_core.events; the StubResponse alias precedes the attribute. Three tests (TestAC001StubResponseHookAttribute) verify (a) the attribute exists and defaults to None, (b) the type-hint introspects to Optional[Callable[[CommandPayload], StubResponse]], and (c) it is writable at runtime so test fixtures can monkeypatch it.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Defines `StubResponse` as a typed dict / Literal union covering `("success", ResultPayload) | ("timeout",) | ("specialist_error", str)`.
criteria_verification.evidence: StubResponse is exposed as a TypeAlias union of three tagged tuples — tuple[Literal['success'], ResultPayload] | tuple[Literal['timeout']] | tuple[Literal['specialist_error'], str] — matching the AC verbatim. Five tests (TestAC002StubResponseAlias) use typing.get_args to verify the alias is exposed, has exactly three variants, and each variant has the required (Literal[tag], payload) shape. ResultPayload is imported from nats_core.events to keep the schema aligned with the real Phase 3 transport.
criteria_verification.notes: StubResponse is exposed as a TypeAlias union of three tagged tuples — tuple[Literal['success'], ResultPayload] | tuple[Literal['timeout']] | tuple[Literal['specialist_error'], str] — matching the AC verbatim. ResultPayload is imported from nats_core.events to keep the schema aligned with the real Phase 3 transport.

criteria_verification.evidence: Both constants are declared at module scope in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py with the exact literal values (no concatenation, no f-string) so they appear unbroken on a single source line each — the prerequisite for the AC-005 grep invariant. Three tests (TestAC003LogPrefixConstants) assert each value via concatenation in the test (`'JARVIS_DISPATCH' + '_STUB'`) so the test file itself does not pollute the grep count, plus a type-check that both are str.
criteria_verification.notes: Both constants are declared at module scope in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py with the exact literal values (no concatenation, no f-string) so they appear unbroken on a single source line each — the prerequisite for the AC-005 grep invariant. Three tests (TestAC003LogPrefixConstants) assert each value via concatenation in the test (`'JARVIS_DISPATCH' + '_STUB'`) so the test file itself does not pollute the grep count, plus a type-check that both are str.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Module docstring carries a "SWAP POINT" section naming the two grep anchors and stating that FEAT-JARVIS-004 replaces `_stub_response_hook` with a real NATS round-trip.
criteria_verification.evidence: The module docstring contains an explicit 'SWAP POINT' section (heading + underline) that enumerates all three named anchors — `_stub_response_hook`, `LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH`, `LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD` — by identifier, and explicitly states 'FEAT-JARVIS-004 replaces `_stub_response_hook` with a real NATS request/reply round-trip (await nats.request(...) on the agents.command.{agent_id} subject)'. Four tests (TestAC004ModuleDocstringSwapPoint) verify (a) the SWAP POINT heading is present, (b) all three anchor names appear in the docstring, (c) FEAT-JARVIS-004 + NATS + 'round-trip' appear, and (d) the docstring deliberately does NOT inline the literal anchor strings (which would break AC-005).
criteria_verification.notes: The module docstring contains an explicit 'SWAP POINT' section (heading + underline) that enumerates all three named anchors — `_stub_response_hook`, `LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH`, `LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD` — by identifier, and explicitly states 'FEAT-JARVIS-004 replaces `_stub_response_hook` with a real NATS request/reply round-trip (await nats.request(...) on the agents.command.{agent_id} subject)'. Four tests (TestAC004ModuleDocstringSwapPoint) verify (a) the SWAP POINT heading is present, (b) all three anchor names appear in the docstring, (c) FEAT-JARVIS-004 + NATS + 'round-trip' appear, and (d) the docstring deliberately does NOT inline the literal anchor strings (which would break AC-005).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `grep -rn "JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB\|JARVIS_QUEUE_BUILD_STUB" src/jarvis/` returns exactly two lines (the two constant definitions) pre-feature wiring; after TASK-J002-013 and TASK-J002-014 land, it returns exactly four (two definitions + two `logger.info` usages).
criteria_verification.evidence: Verified manually: `grep -rIn 'JARVIS_DISPATCH_STUB\|JARVIS_QUEUE_BUILD_STUB' src/jarvis/` returns exactly two lines, both in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py — the LOG_PREFIX_DISPATCH and LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD constant definitions. The literal strings appear nowhere else in src/jarvis/ (Grep tool confirmed 'No matches found' for any other module). Two tests (TestAC005GrepCountPreWiring) use subprocess.run on the actual grep binary to assert (a) src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py contains exactly two grep-anchor lines and (b) the global pre-wiring count is 2 (with a graceful skip path that hands off to TASK-J002-021's stricter post-wiring assertion once Wave 2 lands the dispatch tools).

The literal strings appear nowhere else in src/jarvis/ (Grep tool confirmed 'No matches found' for any other module). Two tests (TestAC005GrepCountPreWiring) use subprocess.run on the actual grep binary to assert (a) src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py contains exactly two grep-anchor lines and (b) the global pre-wiring count is 2 (with a graceful skip path that hands off to TASK-J002-021's stricter post-wiring assertion once Wave 2 lands the dispatch tools).
